primary_meaning: to say you will definitely do something
secondary_meaning: to give grounds for hope or expectation
usage_notes: Follows pattern: promise to do, promise someone something, promise that...
common_collocations: promise to do, make a promise, keep a promise, break a promise
image_search_query: person crossing heart or making pinky promise
image_context: Two children making a pinky promise with serious expressions, or a person crossing their heart
example_1: I promise I'll be there on time.
example_1_translation: Eu prometo que estarei lá na hora.
example_2: She promised to help me with my homework.
example_2_translation: Ela prometeu me ajudar com a lição de casa.
example_3: He made a promise he couldn't keep.
example_3_translation: Ele fez uma promessa que não pôde cumprir.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: PRO-mise. Emphasize first: PRO-mise
memory_hook: Prom-ise - make it 'pro' (for) - a serious commitment
common_mistakes: Not 'promise for doing' - say 'promise to do'

primary_meaning: to tell someone about possible danger or problem
secondary_meaning: to advise someone not to do something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: warn someone about, warn someone not to do, warn that...
common_collocations: warn someone about, warn against, warn not to, early warning
image_search_query: warning sign or person alerting another about danger
image_context: A warning sign on a dangerous road or a person holding up a hand to stop someone from danger
example_1: The weather forecast warns of heavy rain tomorrow.
example_1_translation: A previsão do tempo alerta sobre chuva forte amanhã.
example_2: She warned me about the traffic.
example_2_translation: Ela me alertou sobre o trânsito.
example_3: I warned him not to go alone.
example_3_translation: Eu o avisei para não ir sozinho.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: WARN. Rhymes with 'born' and 'horn'
memory_hook: Warn - like an alarm for your mind
common_mistakes: Not 'warn to someone' - say 'warn someone' or 'warn someone about'

primary_meaning: to ask someone to come to an event
secondary_meaning: to ask someone to do something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: invite someone to, invite someone to do
common_collocations: invite someone to, invite friends, invite someone over
image_search_query: person handing invitation card or waving welcome
image_context: A person presenting a colorful invitation card or waving warmly to welcome guests
example_1: I invited her to my birthday party.
example_1_translation: Eu a convidei para minha festa de aniversário.
example_2: We're inviting all our neighbors over.
example_2_translation: Estamos convidando todos os vizinhos para vir.
example_3: They invited us to stay for dinner.
example_3_translation: Eles nos convidaram para ficar para o jantar.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: in-VITE. Emphasize second: in-VITE
memory_hook: In-vite - 'vite' (quick) way to call people in
common_mistakes: Not 'invite to someone' - say 'invite someone to'

primary_meaning: to give support or confidence
secondary_meaning: to persuade someone to do something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: encourage someone to do, encourage someone in
common_collocations: encourage someone to, encourage someone in, feel encouraged
image_search_query: coach encouraging athlete or friend supporting friend
image_context: A coach giving a thumbs up to an athlete, or a friend putting a hand on another's shoulder with an encouraging smile
example_1: My teacher encouraged me to study abroad.
example_1_translation: Meu professor me encorajou a estudar no exterior.
example_2: She encouraged her son to keep trying.
example_2_translation: Ela encorajou o filho a continuar tentando.
example_3: The good results encouraged us all.
example_3_translation: Os bons resultados nos encorajaram todos.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: en-COUR-age. Emphasize second: en-COUR-age
memory_hook: En-cour-age - give someone more 'courage'
common_mistakes: Not 'encourage for doing' - say 'encourage to do'

primary_meaning: to make someone do something by giving good reasons
secondary_meaning: to convince someone to believe something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: persuade someone to do, persuade someone into doing
common_collocations: persuade someone to, persuade someone into, try to persuade
image_search_query: person making strong argument to convince another
image_context: A salesperson or negotiator making a compelling argument to convince another person of something
example_1: He persuaded me to stay for dinner.
example_1_translation: Ele me convenceu a ficar para o jantar.
example_2: She persuaded her husband to buy a new car.
example_2_translation: Ela convenceu o marido a comprar um carro novo.
example_3: Nothing could persuade her to change her mind.
example_3_translation: Nada poderia convencê-la a mudar de ideia.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: per-SUA-de. Emphasize second: per-SUA-de
memory_hook: Per-suade - through 'suasion' - argue your point through
common_mistakes: Not 'persuade someone for doing' - say 'persuade someone to do'

primary_meaning: to make someone believe something is true
secondary_meaning: to make someone want to do something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: convince someone that, convince someone to do
common_collocations: convince someone that, convince someone to, try to convince
image_search_query: person presenting evidence to convince audience
image_context: A person pointing at evidence or data showing clear proof to convince an audience
example_1: I convinced him that I was right.
example_1_translation: Eu o convenci de que eu estava certo.
example_2: She convinced me to apply for the job.
example_2_translation: Ela me convenceu a me candidatar para o emprego.
example_3: This evidence convinces me of his guilt.
example_3_translation: Esta evidência me convence da culpa dele.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: con-VIN-ce. Emphasize second: con-VIN-ce
memory_hook: Con-vince - 'vince' over doubt, win them over
common_mistakes: Not 'convince someone for something' - say 'convince someone of something' or 'convince someone to do'

primary_meaning: to agree that something is true
usage_notes: Past tense: admitted. Follows pattern: admit something, admit that, admit to doing
common_collocations: admit that, admit to, admit a mistake, admit guilt
image_search_query: person bowing head in admission or confessing
image_context: A person with head bowed slightly, perhaps raising a hand to admit something, honest expression
example_1: I admit that I was wrong.
example_1_translation: Eu admito que estava errado.
example_2: He admitted stealing the money.
example_2_translation: Ele admitiu ter roubado o dinheiro.
example_3: She refused to admit her mistake.
example_3_translation: Ela se recusou a admitir seu erro.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: ad-MIT. Emphasize second: ad-MIT
memory_hook: Ad-mit - let it 'mit' (send) in, acknowledge the truth
common_mistakes: Not 'admit to do' - say 'admit to doing' or 'admit that'

primary_meaning: to say that something is not true
secondary_meaning: to refuse to give someone something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: deny something, deny that, deny doing
common_collocations: deny something, deny that, deny doing, deny access
image_search_query: person shaking head firmly in denial
image_context: A person with firm expression, shaking head from side to side in clear denial
example_1: He denied stealing the money.
example_1_translation: Ele negou ter roubado o dinheiro.
example_2: She denied that she was there.
example_2_translation: Ela nega que estava lá.
example_3: They denied him entry to the country.
example_3_translation: Eles negaram a entrada dele no país.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: de-NY. Emphasize second: de-NY
memory_hook: De-ny - say 'nyet' (no) to it
common_mistakes: Not 'deny to do' - say 'deny doing' or 'deny that'

primary_meaning: to warn that you will hurt or punish someone
secondary_meaning: to seem likely to cause something bad
usage_notes: Follows pattern: threaten someone with, threaten to do, threaten that...
common_collocations: threaten someone with, threaten to do, threaten someone's life
image_search_query: villain pointing finger threatening hero
image_context: A person with angry expression pointing a finger threateningly at another person
example_1: He threatened to call the police.
example_1_translation: Ele ameaçou chamar a polícia.
example_2: She threatened him with a knife.
example_2_translation: Ela o ameaçou com uma faca.
example_3: The storm is threatening our plans.
example_3_translation: A tempestade está ameaçando nossos planos.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: THREAT-en. Emphasize first: THREAT-en
memory_hook: Threat-en - speak 'threats' to someone
common_mistakes: Not 'threaten for doing' - say 'threaten to do' or 'threaten someone with'

primary_meaning: to behave as if something is true when it is not
secondary_meaning: to imagine something that is not real
usage_notes: Follows pattern: pretend to do, pretend that...
common_collocations: pretend to do, pretend that, pretend to be
image_search_query: child pretending to be superhero or doctor playing
image_context: A child dressed up playing imaginary games, pretending to be a superhero or cooking like a chef
example_1: She pretended to be asleep.
example_1_translation: Ela fingiu estar dormindo.
example_2: He pretended that he didn't hear me.
example_2_translation: Ele fingiu que não me ouviu.
example_3: The children are pretending to be pirates.
example_3_translation: As crianças estão fingindo ser piratas.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: pre-TEND. Emphasize second: pre-TEND
memory_hook: Pre-tend - stretch 'pre' (before) the truth
common_mistakes: Not 'pretend like' - say 'pretend that' or 'pretend to be'

primary_meaning: to try to do something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: attempt to do. More formal than 'try'
common_collocations: attempt to do, make an attempt, an attempt at
image_search_query: athlete attempting to jump over high bar
image_context: An athlete about to attempt a high jump, concentration on their face before the crucial moment
example_1: She attempted to climb the mountain.
example_1_translation: Ela tentou subir a montanha.
example_2: He made several attempts to pass the exam.
example_2_translation: Ele fez várias tentativas de passar no exame.
example_3: This is my first attempt at cooking.
example_3_translation: Esta é minha primeira tentativa de cozinhar.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: at-TEMPT. Emphasize second: at-TEMPT
memory_hook: At-tempt - 'tempt' fate by trying
common_mistakes: 'Attempt' is more formal than 'try' - use 'try' in everyday conversation

primary_meaning: to achieve something you tried to do
secondary_meaning: to follow someone in a job
usage_notes: Follows pattern: succeed in doing, succeed with
common_collocations: succeed in, succeed with, succeed at, succeed as
image_search_query: person celebrating success with trophy
image_context: A person raising a trophy in celebration with confetti, expressing triumph and achievement
example_1: She succeeded in passing the exam.
example_1_translation: Ela conseguiu passar no exame.
example_2: He worked hard and succeeded.
example_2_translation: Ele trabalhou duro e conseguiu.
example_3: Nothing succeeds like success.
example_3_translation: Nada succeeds como o sucesso.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: suc-CEED. Emphasize second: suc-CEED
memory_hook: Suc-ceed - 'ceed' (go) after - follow through to achievement
common_mistakes: Not 'succeed to do' - say 'succeed in doing'

primary_meaning: to not succeed in doing something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: fail to do, fail in
common_collocations: fail to do, fail a test, fail an exam, fail at
image_search_query: person looking disappointed after failure
image_context: A person with sad expression, looking down at a failed test paper or dropped items
example_1: He failed the driving test three times.
example_1_translation: Ele reprovou na prova de direção três vezes.
example_2: I failed to understand what she meant.
example_2_translation: Eu não consegui entender o que ela quis dizer.
example_3: Don't be afraid to fail.
example_3_translation: Não tenha medo de falhar.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: FAIL. Rhymes with 'mail' and 'sale'
memory_hook: Fail - the opposite of 'prevail'
common_mistakes: 'Fail' is not just wrong answers - it means not succeeding at all

primary_meaning: to try to win against others
secondary_meaning: to try to get something that others also want
usage_notes: Follows pattern: compete with/against someone, compete in something
common_collocations: compete with, compete against, compete in, compete for
image_search_query: runners at starting line competing in race
image_context: Runners at a starting line in a race, focused expressions, ready to compete against each other
example_1: She competes in international swimming competitions.
example_1_translation: Ela compete em competições internacionais de natação.
example_2: The two companies compete for the same customers.
example_2_translation: As duas empresas competem pelos mesmos clientes.
example_3: Wecompete against our rivals every year.
example_3_translation: Competimos contra nossos rivais todos os anos.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: com-PETE. Emphasize second: com-PETE
memory_hook: Com-pete - 'pet' each other - rivalry
common_mistakes: Not 'compete with someone to do' - say 'compete with someone in' or 'compete to do'

primary_meaning: to be the best or get first place
secondary_meaning: to get something through effort or luck
usage_notes: Past tense: won. Opposite is 'lose'
common_collocations: win a game, win a prize, win money, win the lottery
image_search_query: winner celebrating with trophy or raised hands
image_context: A winner with arms raised in victory holding a trophy, confetti around them
example_1: Our team won the match three to one.
example_1_translation: Nosso time venceu o jogo por três a um.
example_2: She won first prize in the competition.
example_2_translation: Ela ganhou o primeiro prêmio na competição.
example_3: He won a lot of money at the casino.
example_3_translation: Ele ganhou muito dinheiro no cassino.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: WIN. Short 'i' sound, rhymes with 'sin'
memory_hook: Win - you 'win' when you come in first
common_mistakes: Past tense is 'won', not 'winned'

primary_meaning: to not win a game or competition
secondary_meaning: to have something stolen or misplaced
usage_notes: Past tense: lost. Opposite of 'win'
common_collocations: lose a game, lose money, lose weight, lose your way
image_search_query: sad person looking down after losing game
image_context: A person with sad expression looking down, perhaps after a sports loss or misplacing something
example_1: Our team lost the final match.
example_1_translation: Nosso time perdeu a partida final.
example_2: I lost my keys this morning.
example_2_translation: Eu perdi minhas chaves esta manhã.
example_3: She's trying to lose weight.
example_3_translation: Ela está tentando emagrecer.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: LOSE. Long 'oo' sound, rhymes with 'choose'
memory_hook: Lose - you 'lose' when you 'loose' (let go of) something
common_mistakes: 'Lose' (verb) not 'loose' (adjective) - loose means not tight

primary_meaning: to get money for work
secondary_meaning: to deserve something because of your actions
usage_notes: Follows pattern: earn money, earn respect
common_collocations: earn money, earn a living, earn respect, earn interest
image_search_query: worker receiving paycheck or coins
image_context: A worker receiving a paycheck or stacking coins, representing earning money through work
example_1: She earns a good salary.
example_1_translation: Ela ganha um bom salário.
example_2: He earns money by working part-time.
example_2_translation: Ele ganha dinheiro trabalhando meio período.
example_3: Her work earned her everyone's respect.
example_3_translation: O trabalho dela lhe rendeu o respeito de todos.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: EARN. Sounds like 'urn' and 'ernal' without the 'n'
memory_hook: Earn - the 'r' earns its keep in the middle
common_mistakes: 'Earn' not 'ern' - has the 'r' sound

primary_meaning: to give money for something
usage_notes: Past tense: paid. Follows pattern: pay for, pay someone
common_collocations: pay for, pay money, pay by card, pay back
image_search_query: person paying at cash register or handing over money
image_context: A person handing over money or credit card at a cash register, paying for shopping
example_1: I'll pay for the dinner tonight.
example_1_translation: Eu vou pagar o jantar esta noite.
example_2: How would you like to pay?
example_2_translation: Como você gostaria de pagar?
example_3: This job pays well.
example_3_translation: Este trabalho paga bem.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: PAY. Long 'a' sound, rhymes with 'say' and 'day'
memory_hook: Pay - you 'pay' for what you 'say' you want
common_mistakes: 'Pay' for things you buy, 'cost' is what things are worth

primary_meaning: the amount of money needed to buy something
usage_notes: Past tense: cost. 'How much does it cost?' is common question
common_collocations: cost money, how much does it cost, cost a lot, cost someone dearly
image_search_query: price tag showing cost or shopping receipt
image_context: A price tag showing the cost of an item or a receipt with total amount
example_1: How much does this dress cost?
example_1_translation: Quanto custa este vestido?
example_2: The car costs more than I can afford.
example_2_translation: O carro custa mais do que posso pagar.
example_3: Success costs hard work.
example_3_translation: O sucesso custa trabalho duro.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: COST. Short 'o' sound, rhymes with 'lost' and 'frost'
memory_hook: Cost - the 'o' is lost in the middle
common_mistakes: 'Cost' (what something is worth) vs 'spend' (what you pay) - 'It costs €10, I spent €10'

primary_meaning: having a certain value
usage_notes: Use 'worth' + amount: worth €100, worth millions. 'It's worth it' is very common
common_collocations: worth it, worth a lot, be worth, not worth it
image_search_query: gold coin or valuable jewelry representing worth
image_context: A gold coin or sparkling jewelry representing value and worth
example_1: This ring is worth a lot of money.
example_1_translation: Este anel vale muito dinheiro.
example_2: Is this book worth reading?
example_2_translation: Este livro vale a pena ler?
example_3: The trip was expensive but it was worth it.
example_3_translation: A viagem foi cara, mas valeu a pena.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: WORTH. Sounds like 'worth' - similar to 'earth' without the 'e'
memory_hook: Worth - your 'worth' is your value
common_mistakes: Not 'worth to do' - say 'worth doing' or 'worth it'

primary_meaning: having good manners and respect for others
usage_notes: Opposite is 'impolite' or 'rude'
common_collocations: very polite, polite to, polite manner, polite request
image_search_query: person holding door for another showing polite behavior
image_context: A person holding a door open for another with a kind smile, showing polite manners
example_1: She is always polite to everyone.
example_1_translation: Ela é sempre educada com todos.
example_2: Please be polite when you speak to customers.
example_2_translation: Por favor, seja educado quando falar com os clientes.
example_3: It was a polite request.
example_3_translation: Foi um pedido educado.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: po-LITE. Emphasize second: po-LITE
memory_hook: Po-lite - 'lite' (light) behavior - lighten up with good manners
common_mistakes: 'Polite' not 'polit' - ends with 'ite'

primary_meaning: not polite, having bad manners
usage_notes: Opposite is 'polite'
common_collocations: very rude, rude to, rude behavior, it's rude to
image_search_query: person being rude by ignoring or dismissing someone
image_context: A person turning away dismissively or speaking sharply, showing rude behavior
example_1: It was rude of him to interrupt.
example_1_translation: Foi rude da parte dele interromper.
example_2: Don't be rude to your elders.
example_2_translation: Não seja rude com seus superiores.
example_3: His rude behavior upset everyone.
example_3_translation: O comportamento rude dele incomodou todos.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: RUDE. Long 'oo' sound, rhymes with 'food' and 'mood'
memory_hook: Rude - like 'crude' - not refined behavior
common_mistakes: 'Rude' not 'rude' - sounds like 'food' without the 'f'

primary_meaning: telling the truth and not cheating
usage_notes: Opposite is 'dishonest'
common_collocations: very honest, honest person, honest answer, to be honest
image_search_query: person with hand on heart swearing to tell truth
image_context: A person with one hand on their heart, expressing honesty and truthfulness
example_1: She gave an honest answer.
example_1_translation: Ela deu uma resposta honesta.
example_2: To be honest, I don't like the color.
example_2_translation: Para ser honesto, eu não gosto da cor.
example_3: He has an honest face.
example_3_translation: Ele tem um rosto honesto.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: HON-est. Emphasize first: HON-est
memory_hook: Hon-est - 'honor' and 'est' - most honorable
common_mistakes: 'Honest' has 'h' at the start, not silent

primary_meaning: having good luck
usage_notes: Opposite is 'unlucky'
common_collocations: lucky person, lucky charm, lucky day, feel lucky
image_search_query: four-leaf clover or horseshoe symbolizing luck
image_context: A four-leaf clover or horseshoe symbol, or a person with a big happy smile feeling lucky
example_1: I was lucky to find a good parking spot.
example_1_translation: Eu tive sorte de encontrar uma boa vaga de estacionamento.
example_2: She's lucky to have such good friends.
example_2_translation: Ela tem sorte de ter таких хороших друзей.
example_3: Seven is considered a lucky number.
example_3_translation: Sete é considerado um número da sorte.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: LUCK-y. Emphasize first: LUCK-y
memory_hook: Luck-y - full of 'luck'
common_mistakes: 'Lucky' not 'luckey' - ends with 'ky'

primary_meaning: from another country
usage_notes: Often used with 'language', 'country', 'food'
common_collocations: foreign language, foreign country, foreign food, foreign policy
image_search_query: passport with stamps from foreign countries
image_context: A passport with various stamps or a sign showing different foreign languages
example_1: She speaks four foreign languages.
example_1_translation: Ela fala quatro línguas estrangeiras.
example_2: I enjoy foreign films.
example_2_translation: Eu gosto de filmes estrangeiros.
example_3: He's traveling to foreign countries.
example_3_translation: Ele está viajando para países estrangeiros.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: FOR-eign. Emphasize first: FOR-eign
memory_hook: For-eign - 'for' from your own - from outside
common_mistakes: 'Foreign' not 'forign' - has 'e' before 'i'

primary_meaning: existing in nature, not made by people
usage_notes: Opposite is 'artificial' or 'unnatural'
common_collocations: natural beauty, natural disaster, natural resource, natural talent
image_search_query: waterfall in lush green forest showing natural beauty
image_context: A beautiful waterfall surrounded by lush green forest, showing natural beauty
example_1: We enjoyed the natural beauty of the island.
example_1_translation: Aproveitamos a beleza natural da ilha.
example_2: She has a natural talent for music.
example_2_translation: Ela tem um talento natural para música.
example_3: Is this juice natural or artificial?
example_3_translation: Este suco é natural ou artificial?
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: NAT-ur-al. Emphasize first: NAT-ur-al
memory_hook: Nat-ural - from 'nature' - of nature
common_mistakes: 'Natural' not 'naturel' - has 'a' not 'e' after 'r'

primary_meaning: related to medicine and treating illness
usage_notes: Often used with 'treatment', 'care', 'history'
common_collocations: medical treatment, medical care, medical history, medical school
image_search_query: doctor with stethoscope in medical setting
image_context: A doctor in a white coat with a stethoscope, or a medical clinic sign
example_1: She needs medical treatment.
example_1_translation: Ela precisa de tratamento médico.
example_2: He has a medical degree.
example_2_translation: Ele tem um diploma de medicina.
example_3: Medical research has advanced significantly.
example_3_translation: A pesquisa médica avançou significativamente.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: MED-i-cal. Emphasize first: MED-i-cal
memory_hook: Med-i-cal - related to 'medicine'
common_mistakes: 'Medical' not 'medicel' - has 'al' at the end

primary_meaning: liked by many people
usage_notes: Opposite is 'unpopular'
common_collocations: popular music, popular opinion, become popular, popular with
image_search_query: crowd cheering for popular band or celebrity
image_context: A large crowd cheering and waving for a popular band or celebrity on stage
example_1: This is a very popular restaurant.
example_1_translation: Este é um restaurante muito popular.
example_2: She became popular at school.
example_2_translation: Ela se tornou popular na escola.
example_3: Pop music is very popular with young people.
example_3_translation: Música pop é muito popular entre os jovens.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: POP-u-lar. Emphasize first: POP-u-lar
memory_hook: Pop-ular - lots of 'pop' (people) - liked by many
common_mistakes: 'Popular' not 'populer' - has 'a' not 'e' before 'r'

primary_meaning: known by everyone, for everyone
usage_notes: Opposite is 'private'
common_collocations: public library, public transport, public park, public opinion
image_search_query: public library or public park with open access
image_context: A public library building or a public park with people freely using the space
example_1: The public library is on Main Street.
example_1_translation: A biblioteca pública fica na Rua Principal.
example_2: Public transport is convenient here.
example_2_translation: O transporte público é conveniente aqui.
example_3: She doesn't like public speaking.
example_3_translation: Ela não gosta de falar em público.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: PUB-lic. Emphasize first: PUB-lic
memory_hook: Pub-lic - for the 'pub'lic (people) - for everyone
common_mistakes: 'Public' not 'publc' - has 'i' before 'c'

primary_meaning: only for one person or group, not for everyone
usage_notes: Opposite is 'public'
common_collocations: private property, private school, private life, private conversation
image_search_query: private sign or person in private meeting
image_context: A 'private' sign on a door or a person in a private meeting with closed doors
example_1: This is a private conversation.
example_1_translation: Esta é uma conversa privada.
example_2: She goes to a private school.
example_2_translation: Ela estuda em uma escola particular.
example_3: I need some private time.
example_3_translation: Preciso de um tempo privado.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: PRI-vate. Emphasize first: PRI-vate
memory_hook: Pri-vate - 'pri' (prime) - special, not for everyone
common_mistakes: 'Private' not 'privite' - has 'v' before 'a'
